content.problemStatement:
Given a string `S` consisting of only lowercase and uppercase English letters and spaces, your task is to convert it into title case. In title case, the first letter of each word is capitalized while the rest are in lowercase, except for words that are entirely in uppercase (considered as acronyms), which should remain unchanged.

**Note:**


Words are defined as contiguous sequences of English letters separated by spaces.
Acronyms are words that are entirely in uppercase and should remain unchanged.
Assume the input does not contain leading, trailing, or multiple spaces between words.

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer `T`, the number of test cases.
Each of the next `T` lines contains a string `S`.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing the string `S` converted into title case.
constraints: S contains only letters and spaces
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string S consisting of English letters (a‑z, A‑Z) and spaces. There are no leading, trailing, or multiple consecutive spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the string S converted to title case. The first character of each word is uppercase and the remaining characters are lowercase, except for words that are entirely uppercase (acronyms) which remain unchanged.

sampleTestCases.input:
5
hello world
this is a CODECHEF problem
WELCOME to the JUNGLE
the quick BROWN fOx
programming in PYTHON
sampleTestCases.output:
Hello World
This Is A CODECHEF Problem
WELCOME To The JUNGLE
The Quick BROWN Fox
Programming In PYTHON
sampleTestCases.explanation: Each word is processed individually. Words that are completely uppercase (CODECHEF, WELCOME, JUNGLE, BROWN, PYTHON) stay unchanged; all other words are converted to title case.
Single‑letter word "a" becomes "A". "ABC" is an acronym and stays unchanged. In "hello WORLD", "hello" becomes "Hello" while the acronym "WORLD" remains as is.
"cODE" is not all uppercase, so it is title‑cased to "Code". "the" becomes "The". In the third line, "JAVASCRIPT" is an acronym, "is" becomes "Is", and "FUN" stays unchanged.

solution.approach: For each test case we split the input line into words using space as delimiter. For each word we check if it is an acronym (the word equals its uppercase version). If it is, we keep it unchanged. Otherwise we convert the first character to uppercase and the rest to lowercase. Finally we join the processed words with a single space. This runs in linear time with respect to the total length of the input.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total number of characters in the input) per test file
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the output string

code.Java: import java.io.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));String line=br.readLine();int T=Integer.parseInt(line.trim());StringBuilder out=new StringBuilder();for(int t=0;t<T;t++){String s=br.readLine();if(s==null)s="";String[] words=s.split(" ");for(int i=0;i<words.length;i++){String w=words[i];if(!w.isEmpty()&&w.equals(w.toUpperCase())){/* acronym, keep as is */}else if(!w.isEmpty()){w=Character.toUpperCase(w.charAt(0))+w.substring(1).toLowerCase();}out.append(w);if(i+1<words.length)out.append(' ');}if(t+1<T)out.append('\n');}System.out.print(out.toString());}}
